_readme: Generative-image setup for the imagery skill. Copy this to image-generation.config.json (gitignored) and edit. `models` is a set of NAMED entries — `default` picks the one Image with AI falls back to, though normally the skill picks the best model per image TYPE (see src/images/recommend.ts). Everything here is local and key-free EXCEPT the `gemini` backend, which is a cloud call to Google. Bring your own model by adding an entry: any model mflux can run (one mflux CLI per family: mflux-generate, mflux-generate-flux2, mflux-generate-qwen, mflux-generate-z-image, ...) or whatever the Draw Things app has loaded. Without this file the defaults below apply (mflux, FLUX.2 [klein]).
_enabled: Any entry can carry "enabled": false to switch it off — it then stops being offered, recommended, or suggested anywhere (the imagery skill's menu, the console's model picker). Use it for a model you've decided against, so it goes quiet instead of nagging: a model that is merely not installed still gets mentioned ('you don't have FLUX.2, 8/10, installing it would be an upgrade'), which is useful right up until you've ruled it out. Omitted = enabled.

flux2-klein._help: Recommended default: FLUX.2 [klein] 4B (Apache-2.0) — fast, ~13 GB download, strong at readable in-image text. Needs mflux >= 0.18 (`uv tool install mflux` / `uv tool upgrade mflux`); first run downloads weights to ~/.cache/huggingface. 'quantize' (4/8) trades quality for lower memory; 'modelPath' points at a local weights dir; 'extraArgs' appends raw CLI flags.

flux1-schnell._help: The previous default: FLUX.1 [schnell] (Apache-2.0, ~24 GB). Keep it if the weights are already downloaded.

drawthings._help: Runs on the Draw Things app's local API instead of headless mflux — reuses whatever model the app has downloaded and selected ('model' null = app-selected). Enable in Draw Things: Advanced -> API Server -> HTTP, port 7860, localhost.

nano-banana._help: Google's Gemini image model, 'Nano Banana 2'. THE ONE CLOUD ENTRY: your prompt is sent to Google, unlike every other model here. Needs GEMINI_API_KEY in .env — and note image generation is NOT on the API's free tier (that's text-only), so this needs billing enabled on the Google Cloud project; a key without it returns HTTP 429 with limit: 0. Nothing to download. Gemini takes an aspect ratio rather than pixel dimensions, so width/height snap to the nearest supported ratio; set 'aspectRatio'/'imageSize' to pin them. It accepts no seed, so runs are not reproducible. Shipped disabled — flip enabled to true if you have a billed key.

nano-banana-pro._help: Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image) — the higher-end Gemini image model. Same cloud caveat, same key, same billing requirement.
